rules.title: Prohibition of Torture and Inhuman Treatment
rules.summary: Any use of force against a person deprived of liberty that is not strictly necessary diminishes human dignity and violates Art. 3.

rules.title: Right to Liberty and Security
rules.summary: Detention must be lawful, follow a prescribed procedure, and the person must be informed promptly of the reasons.

rules.title: Right to a Fair Trial — Pre-trial Rights
rules.summary: Suspects have the right to legal counsel from the first interrogation and to remain silent.

rules.title: Right to Private Life
rules.source: ECHR Art. 8; S. and Marper v. UK (2008)
rules.summary: Search, seizure of data, and personal interference require legal basis, legitimate aim and proportionality.

rules.title: Charter of Fundamental Rights — Dignity
rules.source: EU Charter of Fundamental Rights, Art. 4
rules.summary: Inhuman or degrading treatment is absolutely prohibited; mirrors ECHR Art. 3 within EU law.

rules.title: Proportionality Principle
rules.source: EU general principle; CJEU case law (e.g., Schrems II)
rules.summary: Police measures must be appropriate, necessary, and not exceed what is required to achieve the legitimate aim.

rules.title: UN Convention Against Torture
rules.summary: Public officials must not inflict severe pain or suffering for punishment, intimidation, or any reason based on discrimination.

rules.title: Italian CPP Art. 385 — Conditions of Arrest
rules.summary: Arrest in flagrante delicto requires the act to be currently in commission or immediately after; otherwise it is unlawful.
